query: modulus of elasticity comparison between polycarboxylate, zincoxyphosphate, gic, and zoe cements

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement has compressive strength equivalent to zinc phosphate cement, whereas its tensile strength is slightly higher than zinc phosphate cement. Type I GIC has lower compressive strength than Type II GIC. GIC is less stiff and has a lower modulus of elasticity comparable to dentin but lesser than zinc phosphate cement. [8] The resilience of GIC is similar to dentin, which is why it is known as man-made dentin.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Mechanical properties such as wear resistance, strength, and fracture toughness are superior to conventional GIC but inferior to composite resins. Compomer bonds micro-mechanically with tooth structure but has no chemical bonding ability. Fluoride release is reduced compared to conventional GIC, but esthetics are superior.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Physical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The thermal expansion coefficient of GIC is similar to enamel and dentin. The thermal diffusivity of GIC is similar to dentin, providing thermal insulation to the pulp. The solubility of GIC in water is higher than zinc phosphate and zinc polycarboxylate. [14] GICs are tooth-colored restorative materials but are not recommended to restore anterior teeth because of their opacity and lack of translucency.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement (GIC) is also known as man-made dentin because it simulates dentin in terms of modulus of elasticity, resilience, coefficient of thermal expansion, and thermal conductivity. GIC chemically adheres to the tooth structure, is biologically compatible, and has fluoride-releasing properties. [4] [5] These features proclaim GIC an excellent substitute for dentin. [7] However, reduced strength, abrasion resistance, and esthetics contribute to its decreased usage in various clinical scenarios. [4] [5] The material selection for a clinical condition should be made after weighing the merits and demerits of the available materials.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Wear resistance, fracture toughness, flexural, and diametral tensile strength of hybrid ionomer are superior to conventional GIC, whereas compressive strength is inferior. [2] [3] The translucency of the hybrid ionomer is superior to conventional GIC. Hybrid ionomer exhibits a slight tendency for shrinkage due to the polymerization of the resin component.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Second Generation
retrieved_docs.content: In this type of cement, the polyacid was already added to the powder, and the setting was achieved by mixing the powder with water or a tartaric acid solution. This GIC is known as water-hardening cement. The main advantages of the second-generation system are enhanced shelf life, decreased initial viscosity, and improved strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties of Metal Modified GIC
retrieved_docs.content: The wear resistance is substantially improved in metal-modified GIC compared to conventional GIC. The wear resistance of cermet is greater than that of silver alloy admixed GIC. The compressive strength and fracture resistance are mildly improved. The use of metal-modified GIC in high-stress-bearing areas is restricted to primary dentition only.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Manipulation
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement can also present as a capsule activated by vibrating it in an auto-mixer. The vibration mixes the cement, and the already mixed GIC paste can be used for the chosen clinical indication.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: The setting of GIC is through an acid-base reaction involving the following stages: Dissolution and decomposition: after mixing the powder and liquid, the acid dissolves the surface of the glass particles to release SiO 4 4- , Ca 2+ , Na + , and F - ions. Initial setting: the Ca 2+ released in the aqueous medium reacts with polyacrylic acid to form a 3-dimensional cross-linked structure. At this time, the material has low strength, and it can be carved using sharp instruments. Final setting: the setting reaction continues during the next 24 hours. During this time, SiO 4 4- forms the silica gel. The slow-moving Al 3+ ions enter the aqueous medium and cross-link the polyacrylic acid chains, displacing the Ca 2+ ions. This increases the final strength of the set cement. Maturation: the aluminum and calcium cross-linked polyacrylate chains get hydrated over time by absorbing water from the aqueous medium. This process is known as maturation. [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The longevity of glass ionomer cement restorations is affected by occlusal forces, porosities in set cement, desiccation or water absorption during initial setting, and use of the mixed cement after the loss of gloss. Furthermore, larger GIC particles decrease the wear rate; the cement's solubility depends on the amount of cement at the margins.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- ZOE Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Absorption, if extruded past the apex Anti-inflammatory effects Antimicrobial effects [5] [9] Disadvantages: Transition into a weak, porous state upon setting Shrinkage upon setting [5] [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The powder-to-liquid ratio determines the mechanical properties of the set cement. More powder increases the consistency of the cement, strength, and speed of the setting reaction. However, the powder-to-liquid ratio can be increased until a critical point. The volume of the physical matrix will be deficient in binding and holding the cement ingredients together; the mechanical properties will be compromised significantly. Thus, the powder-to-liquid ratio should strictly comply with the manufacturer's instructions. Using pre-graduated self-contained mixing capsules solves this problem. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Alginate Impressions -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Impression materials are classified as either elastic or non-elastic based on mechanical properties. [1] Elastic materials are capable of stretching, compressing, and recovering after deformation. Elastic materials comprise reversible and irreversible hydrocolloids, addition and condensation silicones, polysulfides, and polyether. [2] Nonelastic or rigid impression materials include impression compounds, zinc oxide eugenol, and impression waxes. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- The Role of Water in the Setting of GIC
retrieved_docs.content: GIC is water-based cement because water constitutes 11% to 24% of the set cement. Water plays a crucial role in the GIC setting. In the initial phases, it acts as a reaction medium. In later stages, it mediates the slow hydration of the cross-linked chains.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Polyacid Modified Glass Ionomer/Compomer
retrieved_docs.content: Compomer is formed by combining composite resin with glass ionomer. In this material, ion-leachable glass particles of GIC are added as fillers in the composite matrix. This combination integrates some of the composite resins and glass ionomer cement properties. Compomer is supplied as a single-component material. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Bonding
retrieved_docs.content: GIC chemically (ionic) bonds to the tooth structure (enamel and dentin). GIC bonds better to enamel than dentin because of the higher inorganic content in enamel. The mechanism of GIC adhesion to the tooth's inorganic structure involves a chelation reaction between the carboxyl groups of the polyacrylic acid and the calcium in the hydroxyapatite crystals of the tooth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1827
retrieved_docs.content: Replacement preparation is a therapeutic formulation that incorporates kalcium-glycerolfosfát and sodium hyaluronate as its constituent components. Kalcium-glycerolfosfát provides a calcium‑phosphate source that can support mineralization processes, while sodium hyaluronate contributes viscoelastic and lubricating properties useful for tissue repair and joint function. By combining these two agents, the replacement preparation is designed to enhance clinical outcomes in procedures that require both structural support and improved biomechanical performance.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1918
retrieved_docs.content: Replacement preparation is a medical formulation that incorporates several specific components, including kalcium‑glycerolfosfát, dibasic potassium phosphate, and sodium hyaluronate. Kalcium‑glycerolfosfát contributes calcium and phosphate ions that can support mineral balance, while dibasic potassium phosphate provides additional phosphate and potassium ions to help maintain physiological pH and electrolyte homeostasis. Sodium hyaluronate, a high‑molecular‑weight glycosaminoglycan, offers viscoelastic properties that are valuable for joint lubrication and tissue repair. Together, these ingredients define the composition of the replacement preparation, highlighting its role in delivering essential minerals and lubricating agents for therapeutic use.

retrieved_docs.content: material rather than a therapeutic agent. The mineral “perleť” (mother‑of‑pearl) is also linked to calcium carbonate, reflecting that perleť is a natural polymorph of calcium carbonate and thus conceptually a “child” of the calcium carbonate entity. Overall, calcium carbonate appears across a spectrum of products: as an essential calcium source in human multivitamins and digestive enzyme blends, and as an inert excipient in veterinary antibiotic preparations, while also being recognized as the underlying chemical basis of natural materials such as perleť. This dual role underscores calcium carbonate’s versatility in pharmaceutical formulation, contributing both to therapeutic efficacy and to the physical properties of dosage forms.
